positive_signals: semantic coloring (based on structure) is useful
color coding improves readability and tracking of hashes
hex dumps win due to familiarity
basic syntax highlighting can make a difference in readability
negative_signals: it’s a pretty basic hex viewer
can’t think of a better representation than a hex dump
random colors at each byte is hard to identify specific values
unique bytes have no unique meaning across data types
sentiment.justification: Commenters generally agree on the value of color coding in hex editors, but some express concern about the quality and usability of specific implementations, while others suggest additional features or alternative approaches for visualizing binary data.
